edges.description: Loss of PSMD12 reduces proteasome function and perturbs protein homeostasis.
Proteasome dysfunction perturbs growth and organelle-homeostasis pathways.
PSMD12 loss is associated with an interferon-stimulated transcriptional response in patient cells.
Altered developmental growth control contributes to delayed milestones.
Neurodevelopmental disruption contributes to persistent cognitive impairment.
Developmental pathway disruption contributes to delayed communication milestones.
Perturbed neurodevelopmental signaling contributes to autism spectrum features.
Dysregulated morphogenesis contributes to craniofacial dysmorphism.
Disturbed developmental programs contribute to skeletal anomalies.
Congenital malformations can involve the heart.
Congenital malformations can involve the kidneys.
Altered developmental growth control contributes to reduced stature.
Later-childhood metabolic dysregulation may contribute to obesity.

nodes.description: Craniofacial dysmorphism is a core feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Haploinsufficiency of PSMD12, which encodes a subunit of the core component of the proteasome, causes Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome (STISS), characterized by intellectual disability, autism spectrum disorder, craniofacial dysmorphisms, with or without other congenital anomalies, and autoinflammation.

nodes.description: Cardiac anomalies are part of the congenital anomaly spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: In addition to previously reported DD/ID, speech delay, cardiac and renal anomalies, we also confirmed preaxial hand abnormalities as a feature of this syndrome.

nodes.description: Renal anomalies have been reported in STISS.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: In addition to previously reported DD/ID, speech delay, cardiac and renal anomalies, we also confirmed preaxial hand abnormalities as a feature of this syndrome.

nodes.description: Skeletal anomalies are a recurring component of STISS.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All individuals presented with developmental delay, intellectual disability, craniofacial, and skeletal anomalies.

nodes.description: Autism spectrum disorder is part of the described PSMD12 phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Haploinsufficiency of PSMD12, which encodes a subunit of the core component of the proteasome, causes Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome (STISS), characterized by intellectual disability, autism spectrum disorder, craniofacial dysmorphisms, with or without other congenital anomalies, and autoinflammation.

nodes.description: Speech delay is repeatedly reported in STISS.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: In addition to previously reported DD/ID, speech delay, cardiac and renal anomalies, we also confirmed preaxial hand abnormalities as a feature of this syndrome.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a core manifestation of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: In addition to previously reported DD/ID, speech delay, cardiac and renal anomalies, we also confirmed preaxial hand abnormalities as a feature of this syndrome.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is a core cognitive phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All individuals presented with developmental delay, intellectual disability, craniofacial, and skeletal anomalies.

nodes.description: Obesity can emerge later in childhood in STISS.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Most subjects had acne, short stature, and developed obesity since late childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: We report 24 additional unrelated patients with STISS with various truncating single nucleotide variants or copy-number variant deletions involving PSMD12.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We described six patients (four adults) from two unrelated families carrying a known p.(Arg289*) or a novel p.(Tyr111*) PSMD12 variant.
PSMD12 | HGNC:9557 | Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome | MONDO:0054591 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Heterozygous loss-of-function variants in PSMD12 reduce gene dosage during development.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Haploinsufficiency of PSMD12 has been reported in individuals with neurodevelopmental phenotypes, including developmental delay/intellectual disability (DD/ID), facial dysmorphism, and congenital malformations, defined as Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome (STISS).

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Alteration in the ubiquitin-proteasome system results in human disorders with neurological and/or autoinflammatory presentation.

nodes.description: Reduced proteasome activity perturbs growth, organelle-homeostasis, and inflammatory pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Alteration in the ubiquitin-proteasome system results in human disorders with neurological and/or autoinflammatory presentation.

nodes.description: Short stature is present in a subset of reported patients.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Most subjects had acne, short stature, and developed obesity since late childhood.

nodes.description: Patient cells show induction of type I interferon-stimulated genes, which provides a molecular biomarker for the syndrome and a clue to immune dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Remarkably, our data show that STISS patient cells exhibit a profound remodeling of the mTORC1 and mitophagy pathways with an induction of type I interferon-stimulated genes.

evidence.reference_title: Does It Run in Your Family? Inherited Truncating PSMD12 Variants Broaden the Phenotypic Spectrum of Stankiewicz-Isidor Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: None presented clinical manifestations of autoinflammation and the detected IFN-I signature perturbations were not specific.

nodes.description: STISS patient cells show remodeling of mTORC1 and mitophagy pathways, linking proteostasis defects to altered cellular growth and quality-control programs.

evidence.reference_title: Stankiewicz-Isidor syndrome: expanding the clinical and molecular phenotype.
evidence.snippet: Remarkably, our data show that STISS patient cells exhibit a profound remodeling of the mTORC1 and mitophagy pathways with an induction of type I interferon-stimulated genes.
